LAW AND MOTION 301, DEFENDANTS JAMES D. KLEIN AND UBS FINANCIAL SERVICES, INC.'S MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION: 1) MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION IS GRANTED AS TO THE 1ST, 2ND, 3RD AND 6TH CAUSES OF ACTION; 2) MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION IS DENIED AS TO THE 4TH (ASSAULT) AND 5TH (INTERFERENCE WITH COTNRACTS) CAUSES OF ACTION; 3) ACTION IS STAYED PENDING COMPLETION OF THE ARBITRATION. Off Calendar Mar-17-2004 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
RULING:
NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DEFENDANTS JAMES D. KLEIN and UBS FINANCIAL SERVICES, INC.'S Motion To Compel Arbitration: 1) Motion To Compel Arbitration is GRANTED as to the 1st, 2nd, 3rd and 6th Causes of Action; 2) Motion to Compel Arbitration is DENIED as to the 4th (assault) and 5th (interference with cotnracts) Causes of Action; 3) Action is STAYED pending completion of the arbitration. UBS is to pay the arbitration fees as offered in their reply. Prevailing party to submit form of order. (301/AJR/MT-X) Reporter: Joyce Long, CSR #8620

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
